target.title: fix(webchat): recover session after stop
target.body:
Fixes #91987.

## Summary
- Persist successful WebChat `chat.abort` requests as terminal `killed` session lifecycle before the RPC responds, including `abortedLastRun: true`, `endedAt`, and `runtimeMs`.
- Reconcile WebChat locally after a confirmed Stop so the active run id/stream/tool state are cleared even if the terminal chat event is delayed or lost.
- Guard both new abort terminalization paths against stale run races: gateway persistence now refuses to overwrite a newer session generation, and the client only reconciles the captured run id when the abort response confirms that run.
- Add regression coverage for stale `running` session rows, run-scoped abort persistence, local Stop recovery, unconfirmed abort responses, and stale local abort responses.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Clicking Stop during an active WebChat run no longer leaves the selected session stuck as `status: "running"` / `abortedLastRun: false`, and a stale Stop response cannot clear a newer same-session run.

- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw worktree at `/Users/andy/openclaw-91987-webchat-stop-recovery` on branch `codex/91987-webchat-stop-recovery`, head `0554e3b8c8b238deaa17f246863f2ae25ef1d67c`, with dependencies installed from `pnpm-lock.yaml` via `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: I ran the issue acceptance commands, the review-feedback regressions, and the local changed-file gate after the latest patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/run-lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.abort-persistence.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`

- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured from the local OpenClaw worktree after the latest patch:

  ```text
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  86 passed (86)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 1.64s

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  120 passed (120)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 2.28s

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/run-lifecycle.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  12 passed (12)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 1.26s

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts
  Test Files  4 passed (4)
  Tests  108 passed (108)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.08s

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.abort-persistence.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  56 passed (56)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 16.18s

  $ OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed
  [check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
  [check:changed] typecheck core
  [check:changed] typecheck core tests
  [check:changed] lint core changed files
  Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
  runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The gateway regression proves an abort for an older run cannot rewrite a newer `running` session row, while the run-scoped abort regression still proves the matching row becomes `killed` with `abortedLastRun: true` before `chat.abort` replies. The WebChat controller regressions prove a confirmed Stop clears `chatRunId`/`chatStream` and preserves partial assistant text, while unconfirmed or stale Stop responses leave the current run untouched.

- What was not tested: Browser-driven live provider generation was not available from this shell. A separate real WebChat browser/provider Stop-then-send proof pass should still capture the visible Stop action, the immediate next prompt, and the second turn completing without refresh or `/reset`.
target.author: turbotheturtle
